per_turn_leaks.student_message: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Y-yes, it has. And it's not just the pain, doc. I've also been having bloody stools with mucus in them, which is terrifying me. The blood is bright red and mixed in with my stool, and it's happening more often than ever.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, doc... after I have a bowel movement, especially when there's blood in it, I feel this overwhelming sense of urgency and discomfort, like my stomach is still trying to get rid of something. And sometimes, even after I've gone, I feel like I didn't completely empty my bowels, which makes me anxious all over again.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
Doc, actually, the only significant medical condition I had in the past was an appendectomy when I was 16 years old, and that went smoothly without any complications. Other than that, I'm relatively healthy, although my older sister did get diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, which is a bit concerning for me given our family history.

However, I do want to mention that I haven't seen a doctor in over two years, and I haven't had any recent bloodwork or tests done. That might be relevant to understanding what's going on with my symptoms.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Doc, yes... I'm a bit of an occasional drinker, I'd say. I usually have 2-3 beers most weeknights when I unwind after work, but over the past month, I've actually increased my intake to 4-5 beers per night due to stress from symptoms and work. It's not something I'm proud of, but it's been a way for me to cope with everything that's been going on.
